home.message:
We can’t wait to share this special day with you.
 Come celebrate a late-summer night with us.

travel.title: Travel
travel.message:
We know that traveling for a destination wedding can take extra time and planning, and we completely understand if you’re not able to join us in person. We are so grateful for your love and well wishes.

If you’re able to make the trip, we’re so excited to celebrate together! Below you’ll find directions to the venue — Hotel Wailea, and a few places to stay.
travel.flightsBody: Kahului Airport (OGG) is the main airport on Maui. Wailea is about a 25–30 minute drive from the airport.
travel.flightsLayover: There are not many direct flights to Maui from most cities — you'll likely connect through Honolulu (HNL) before a short 45-minute hop to Kahului.

gettingAround.heading: Rental Car
gettingAround.body: If you're planning to explore the island before or after the wedding, we recommend renting a car so you can fully enjoy Maui's beaches, scenic drives, and hidden gems.

gettingAround.heading: Rideshare
gettingAround.body: If you'd rather keep things simple, Uber and Lyft are available and can take you directly to the venue and around Wailea.

hotel.name: Hotel Wailea
hotel.description: We're getting married at Hotel Wailea, and have special discounted rates for our guests. Book through the Hotel Wailea website and enter the promo code below. The 25% discount code is valid for stays between August 25 - September 2, 2026.
hotel.note: Please note that Hotel Wailea's prices have increased more than we initially anticipated. We've noticed that Airbnb tends to offer better pricing in the Wailea area, so we'd recommend looking into that as an option as well. Whatever you choose, we'd love for everyone to stay close by and make a weekend of it!

travel.altHotelsNote: If you're looking for other options, there are lots of great places to stay nearby in Wailea too. We've found that Airbnb often has more affordable options in the area — whatever works best for you!
tips: Late August in Maui is warm and sunny — expect temperatures around 85°F (29°C).
Don't forget reef-safe sunscreen! Hawaii law requires it to protect the coral reefs.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.subtitle: We'll share the final timeline closer to the wedding, but here is the expected schedule.
schedule.venue: Hotel Wailea, 555 Kaukahi Street, Wailea, HI

registry.title: Registry
registry.message: Your presence at our wedding is the greatest gift of all. If you wish to honor us with a gift, we've created a small registry.

invite.message:
We can't wait to share this special day with you.
 Come celebrate a late-summer night with us.
invite.alreadyRsvp: If you have already completed the RSVP, you can view  more information about the wedding such as hotel discount, travel tips, and more about the couple.

storyBook.title: Our Story
storyBook.subtitle: The chapters that led us here
storyBook.tapHint: Tap the photos to read from Jaicob's side

captions.001: I was curious about her, she seemed shy at first, I probably was too. I could barely walk at the time due to an injury but I didn't want the date to end so I powered through and we walked through the neighborhood to dinner. By the end of the day I felt I got a glimpse into who she is and I wanted to see her again.
captions.002: You know what they say about distance. When either of us returned it was a happy moment. She brought me back a chess board, and I got to see her competitive charm for the first time.
captions.003: I managed to perfectly balance my car on a rock. It was an embarrassing moment, but she did not judge me for it and we were able to laugh it off over udon later that day.
captions.004: We stumbled across a bluegrass festival in Golden Gate Park, and everything felt perfect.
captions.005: We had our challenges. Both of us come from different backgrounds and with different experiences and different points of view. I felt connected to this person though and in Dana's words "we make a good team." We had fun together. I wanted to keep that going.
captions.006: It felt natural travelling together. I finally got to feel the romance of being in NYC during the holidays with someone special.
captions.007: I wanted to show her the beauty of California and…
captions.008: To give her a memorable birthday experience to express that I wanted to be with her for a long time.
captions.009: For my birthday she planned a whole day of puzzles, activities and special moments. I was truly touched. I've never experienced anything like it before. It's one of my most cherished memories.
captions.011: We attended a wedding together in Greece. I was in awe of the surrounding beauty and feeling inspired about the future, our future.
captions.012: Portugal felt like our first big adventure. Exploring, indulging, and admiring. I remember watching her walk up a hill to our next hotel and feeling love for her.
captions.013a: Some days you feel down for no reason
captions.013b: Usually it means you just need to go outside
captions.014: I was curious to see how Dana would do travelling with my family. They can be a handful..
captions.015: She fit right in. We shared KBBQ with my parents and caught last minute tickets to go see Les Mis with my little sister.
captions.016: In York I again watched Dana walk ahead of me, feeling love for her. We passed a shop with engagement rings and it crossed my mind that maybe I should buy one.
captions.017: Winding our trip we watched a fire-like sunset that will forever be vividly burned into my memory.
captions.018a: We arrived late to our cabin and it was this night I learned of the determination of Dana's midnight cravings. The steak was good though.
captions.018b: Watching Dana react to Yosemite with tears motivated me more than ever to show her all the beautiful things in the world.
captions.019: Saying goodbye every weekend was getting harder and harder. I was excited to start building a life together and invited Dana to move in.
captions.020: My first visit to Korea I was nervous, curious, excited, and relieved. Dana has a lovely family. I felt a sense of wonder thinking about the idea of having another place I could call home
captions.021: Listening to live music sitting among the Cherry Blossoms; A real life K-drama! It was a different kind of beauty than I've ever experienced.
captions.022a: The pork was average but the vibes were 20/10.
captions.022b: I often look forward to more of these nights.
captions.023: In Japan we both felt equally lost. It was a hard day getting to this hotel but the goofy photoshoot we had was worth the struggle. It's good to have someone by your side when everything feels unfamiliar. "We make a good team."
captions.024: My lowest point, and honestly a blur. I felt cared for though and grateful to the entire Choi family.
captions.025: We had to buckle down. It was one of the hardest years for both of us full of work, uncertainty and change. I was grateful I did not have to go through it alone and that we were able to support each other's goals. Turns out Dana makes a great business advisor!
captions.026: Just a few months before meeting Dana I came to this spot and thought to myself that it'd be a perfect place to propose to someone one day.

chapters.label: Chapter One
chapters.title: The Day We Met
chapters.text: When I left New York and moved to San Francisco, I thought to myself that I should meet a "tech guy." When we first met, I don't think either of us attached much meaning to it. But before we met, Jaicob told me, "My mission is to make you fall in love with San Francisco," and that secretly made me excited. Our first date was simple — coffee in the park that turned into wine, and dinner — but somehow the day flew by in an instant.

chapters.label: Chapter Two
chapters.title: More Than Friends
chapters.text: The phase when we were something between friends and not-quite-friends. After meeting Jaicob, I went on a two-week trip to Argentina. Later he traveled to Europe for two weeks with his family and then I went back to Korea during the summer. There were quite a few stretches when we were apart. And yet, somehow, we kept finding our way back to each other. I brought back a chess set from Argentina, and Jaicob gave me a painting and a bracelet from Europe. They felt like small tokens proving that we had been thinking of each other during our travels.

chapters.label: Chapter Three
chapters.title: Making It Official
chapters.text: We officially became a couple. On a rainy day, very casually, Jaicob asked, "Will you be my girlfriend?" At the time, we had no idea what we would end up facing together in the future…

chapters.label: Chapter Four
chapters.title: The Holidays
chapters.text: Christmas, my birthday, Jaicob's birthday, and Valentine's Day were all clustered together. Under warm yellow lights and a festive atmosphere, time passed quickly. We still weren't at a place where we could easily open up about deeper feelings, so we didn't really know how to untangle awkwardness or discomfort. As a couple of shy people it's something we would continue to work on together.

chapters.label: Chapter Five
chapters.title: First Trip Abroad
chapters.text: On our first long-distance trip together, we experienced both new joys and discomfort at the same time. After a 17-hour flight and in unfamiliar surroundings, we learned more about what each of us liked and disliked. For me, it was my first time back in Europe in ten years, so it felt especially emotional. Walking again through places that had shaped my life, this time with Jaicob, brought a deep sense of nostalgia. I was grateful that we could eat the same food and look out at the same views in Greece and Portugal.

chapters.label: Chapter Six
chapters.title: Growing Closer
chapters.text: We traveled with Jaicob's family for ten days. The trip started in Atlanta and continued through London, Edinburgh, York, and Hampshire. We know that we will never forget the unbelievably fiery red sky we saw in Hampshire. Little by little, we began to blend into each other's families and everyday lives.

chapters.label: Chapter Seven
chapters.title: Dana's World
chapters.text: I introduced "my world" to Jaicob. Even though he already enjoyed Korean food and watched Korean TV series with me, I wanted to properly show him Korean culture. He met my family, and we shared late-night samgyeopsal and soju at a pocha. Ordinary moments of my life in Korea became something special.

chapters.label: Chapter Eight
chapters.title: Side by Side
chapters.text: A time when we focused on our individual goals. I started a new project and became busy, and Jaicob was also super busy with re-building his business. The main thing I could do for him was cook — and that's when my cooking skills improved a lot. Under the same roof, we struggled side by side chasing our own goals. Through the hard times, we felt more certain that no matter what happens, we could always support and lean on each other.

chapters.label: Chapter Nine
chapters.title: The Beginning of Forever
chapters.text: These past few years have been full of highs and lows, special moments and challenges but undoubtedly the best years of my life so far. This journey is truly different for everyone. It takes determination and relentless willingness to understand one another. I love who we are becoming together, the fun we have, the life we are building. I look forward to doing it forever, wherever that takes us.
